papers.authors: Shekhar S, Bhagat K, Padhi AK
papers.title: FimH-mediated adhesion across uroplakin glycan contexts: a structural and computational integration
papers.annotation: Comprehensive structural and molecular dynamics analysis linking FimH mutations to glycan-binding outcomes across oligomannose conformational states and uroplakin-receptor interactions.

papers.title: A comprehensive protein design protocol to identify resistance mutations and signatures of adaptation in pathogens
papers.annotation: Describes the broader ResScan-style design logic that informs the mutation-screening workflow used in this project.

papers.title: Catch-bond mechanism of the bacterial adhesin FimH
papers.annotation: Defines the force-dependent conformational logic behind FimH catch-bond behavior and is central to interpreting shear-enhanced adhesion.

papers.title: Structural basis of tropism of Escherichia coli to the bladder during urinary tract infection
papers.annotation: Explains bladder-specific mannose recognition and highlights several core mannose-binding-pocket residues used as anchors for later mutation analysis.

papers.title: Intervening with Urinary Tract Infections Using Anti-Adhesives Based on the Crystal Structure of the FimH–Oligomannose-3 Complex
papers.annotation: Provides the OM3-bound FimH structural template that underpins the human OM3 modeling workflow used in this platform.

papers.title: Structural insights into a cooperative switch between one and two FimH bacterial adhesins binding pauci- and high-mannose type N-glycan receptors
papers.annotation: Introduces a key OM6 structural reference and clarifies how extended high-mannose glycans change the FimH binding landscape.

papers.title: The affinity of the FimH fimbrial adhesin is receptor-driven and quasi-independent of Escherichia coli pathotypes
papers.annotation: A key reference for naturally occurring FimH variation, including the N135K loss-of-binding state used as an important comparison point.

papers.title: Cryo-EM elucidates the uroplakin complex structure within liquid-crystalline lipids in the porcine urothelial membrane
papers.annotation: Supplies the porcine uroplakin structural scaffold and the membrane-context framing for host-side receptor architecture.

papers.title: Uroplakin Ia is the urothelial receptor for uropathogenic Escherichia coli: evidence from in vitro FimH binding
papers.annotation: One of the foundational receptor studies linking UPK1A directly to FimH-mediated attachment at the urothelial surface.

papers.title: Differentiation and developmental pathways of uropathogenic Escherichia coli in urinary tract pathogenesis
papers.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
papers.annotation: Defines the intracellular bacterial community program that underlies persistence and recurrence in UPEC infection.

papers.title: Global, regional, and national burden of urinary tract infections from 1990 to 2019: an analysis of the Global Burden of Disease study 2019
papers.annotation: Provides the recent epidemiological basis for the global UTI burden figures used across the site.
